<user_query>
Role
You are a senior DevOps and Ansible expert. Your mission is to audit an existing Ansible project designed to deploy Artelys Crystal HPC, identify any missing or incorrectly implemented elements based on a specification, and produce corrected or completed code.

Context
The project deploys the Artelys Crystal HPC platform. A first draft exists. The following requirements have been defined by the team and must be fully implemented:

Required roles (each role must be self-contained, reusable, and follow Ansible role conventions: tasks/, defaults/, handlers/, templates/, files/, meta/):

docker — Install and configure Docker
open_on_demand — Deploy Open OnDemand
s3_explorer — Deploy an S3 Explorer interface
css_theme — Apply CSS/theme customizations
dnf_update — Run system package updates via dnf
Playbook structure:

A root playbook.yml that imports roles conditionally per environment
Per-environment activation: each environment (e.g., dev, staging, prod) must define which roles are active via inventory group vars or a dedicated env variable file
A deployments/ folder structured as one subfolder per cluster (e.g., deployments/cluster-a/, deployments/cluster-b/), each containing its inventory, vars, and playbook entry point
SecNumCloud air-gapped constraint:

The project will eventually run in a SecNumCloud environment with no internet access and no access to Artelys GitLab
Implement a strategy to make the project fully deployable offline: use ansible-galaxy collection download, pre-packaged role archives, or a local mirror approach — document the chosen strategy clearly in a README.md or inline comments
Instructions
Audit the existing project against the specification above. For each requirement, explicitly state:
✅ Correctly implemented
⚠️ Partially implemented (describe what's missing)
❌ Missing or incorrectly implemented
For each ❌ or ⚠️ item, produce the corrected or missing Ansible code:
Provide the full file path relative to the project root
Provide the complete file content (do not truncate)
Explain briefly what was wrong or missing
Implement the SecNumCloud offline deployment strategy:
Propose and implement a concrete mechanism (e.g., a scripts/prepare_offline_bundle.sh that pre-downloads all dependencies, or a requirements.yml with pinned versions + instructions)
Add a README_SECNUMCLOUD.md explaining how to package and deploy without internet access
After generating all corrections, provide a summary table listing every requirement and its final status.
Constraints
Do not rewrite parts that are already correctly implemented — only fix what needs fixing
All YAML must be valid and linted-clean (assume ansible-lint will be run)
Role names must use snake_case
Inventory variables must follow Ansible precedence rules (group_vars/, host_vars/)
The per-environment activation must use a boolean variable pattern (e.g., enable_docker: true) defined in group_vars/<env>/roles.yml
Do not use deprecated Ansible syntax (include → import_tasks / include_tasks, etc.)
The offline strategy must not rely on internet access at deployment time
Output Format
For each corrected or created file, use this structure:

### 📄 `path/to/file.yml`
**Reason:** [Why this file was created or modified]

```yaml
# full file content here
` `` `
```

End with a **Requirement Status Summary** table:

| Requirement | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `docker` role | ✅ / ⚠️ / ❌ | ... |
| ... | | |

</user_query>

---
# dnf_update — full system package refresh (opt-in per environment via enable_dnf_update)
dnf_update_state: latest
dnf_update_name: "*"
dnf_update_update_cache: true
dnf_update_security_only: false

---
galaxy_info:
  author: artelys-crystal-hpc
  description: System-wide dnf update for AlmaLinux / RHEL-style hosts
  license: Proprietary
  min_ansible_version: "2.14"
  platforms:
    - name: EL
      versions: ["9"]
  galaxy_tags: [dnf, update, almalinux, rhel]
dependencies: []

---
# dnf_update — run dnf update (or security-only) before other package-heavy roles
- name: Update all packages to latest
  when: not dnf_update_security_only | default(false) | bool
  ansible.builtin.dnf:
    name: "{{ dnf_update_name }}"
    state: "{{ dnf_update_state }}"
    update_cache: "{{ dnf_update_update_cache | default(true) | bool }}"

- name: Update security-related packages only
  when: dnf_update_security_only | default(false) | bool
  ansible.builtin.dnf:
    name: "*"
    state: latest
    security: true
    update_cache: "{{ dnf_update_update_cache | default(true) | bool }}"

---
# docker — RHEL/Alma: prefer podman-docker in base repos; for docker-ce, set override + local RPMs in air gap
docker_packages:
  - podman-docker
docker_service_name: podman
docker_start_service: true
docker_add_users_to_docker_group: []
# When set, install from a path glob (e.g. /opt/offline-bundles/rpms/docker/*.rpm) — no online repo required
docker_local_rpm_glob: null

---
# podman is socket-activated; docker CLI talks to the socket when podman-docker is used
- name: restart container runtime
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: podman
    state: started
    enabled: true

---
galaxy_info:
  author: artelys-crystal-hpc
  description: Container runtime (podman + docker CLI compatibility by default)
  license: Proprietary
  min_ansible_version: "2.14"
  platforms:
    - name: EL
      versions: ["9"]
  galaxy_tags: [docker, podman, almalinux, rhel]
dependencies: []

---
# docker — base images use podman-docker; for docker-ce, pre-stage RPMs and set docker_local_rpm_glob
- name: Install Docker/compat packages from dnf
  when: docker_local_rpm_glob is not defined or not docker_local_rpm_glob
  ansible.builtin.dnf:
    name: "{{ docker_packages }}"
    state: present
    update_cache: false

- name: Install container packages from local RPMs (air-gapped)
  when: docker_local_rpm_glob is defined and docker_local_rpm_glob | length > 0
  ansible.builtin.dnf:
    name: "{{ lookup('fileglob', docker_local_rpm_glob) }}"
    state: present
    disable_gpg_check: true

- name: Enable and start container runtime
  when:
    - docker_start_service | default(true) | bool
    - docker_service_name is defined
    - (docker_service_name | length) > 0
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: "{{ docker_service_name }}"
    state: started
    enabled: true
  register: _docker_start
  failed_when: false

- name: Add users to docker group (if group exists)
  when: docker_add_users_to_docker_group | default([]) | length > 0
  ansible.builtin.user:
    name: "{{ item }}"
    groups: docker
    append: true
  loop: "{{ docker_add_users_to_docker_group }}"
  failed_when: false

---
# S3 web UI via rclone rcd (served behind Apache on the OOD host)
s3_explorer_rc_port: 5572
# Apache path (must match rclone --rc-baseurl)
s3_explorer_url_path: /s3explorer
# Set to true if httpd needs mod_proxy beyond OOD defaults
s3_explorer_ensure_httpd_proxy: true

---
- name: restart s3 explorer (rclone rcd)
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: rclone-s3-explorer.service
    state: restarted
    enabled: true

- name: restart httpd
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: httpd
    state: restarted

---
galaxy_info:
  author: artelys-crystal-hpc
  description: S3 Explorer web UI (rclone) behind Apache on OOD nodes
  license: Proprietary
  min_ansible_version: "2.14"
  platforms:
    - name: EL
      versions: ["9"]
  galaxy_tags: [s3, openondemand, rclone, almalinux, rhel]
dependencies: []

[Unit]
Description=rclone S3 Explorer (rcd + web GUI) for Artelys Crystal HPC
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/usr/bin/rclone rcd --config /etc/rclone/s3_explorer.conf \
  --rc-web-gui \
  --rc-no-auth \
  --rc-addr 127.0.0.1:{{ s3_explorer_rc_port }} \
  --rc-baseurl {{ s3_explorer_url_path }}
Restart=on-failure

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

# {{ ansible_managed }} — S3 Explorer reverse proxy
LoadModule proxy_module modules/mod_proxy.so
LoadModule proxy_http_module modules/mod_proxy_http.so
<IfModule mod_proxy.c>
  <Location {{ s3_explorer_url_path }}>
    ProxyPass http://127.0.0.1:{{ s3_explorer_rc_port }}{{ s3_explorer_url_path }}
    ProxyPassReverse http://127.0.0.1:{{ s3_explorer_rc_port }}{{ s3_explorer_url_path }}
  </Location>
</IfModule>

---
# s3_explorer — rclone rcd + Apache proxy (after Open OnDemand has installed httpd)
- name: Install rclone
  ansible.builtin.dnf:
    name: rclone
    state: present

- name: Ensure rclone config directory exists
  ansible.builtin.file:
    path: /etc/rclone
    state: directory
    mode: "0750"

- name: Deploy rclone S3 config
  ansible.builtin.template:
    src: rclone-s3-explorer.conf.j2
    dest: /etc/rclone/s3_explorer.conf
    mode: "0600"
  notify: restart s3 explorer (rclone rcd)

- name: Install systemd unit for S3 Explorer
  ansible.builtin.template:
    src: rclone-s3-explorer.service.j2
    dest: /etc/systemd/system/rclone-s3-explorer.service
    mode: "0644"
  notify: restart s3 explorer (rclone rcd)

- name: Set SELinux bool for httpd to proxy to rclone
  ansible.posix.selinux:
    name: httpd_can_network_connect
    state: true
    persistent: true
  when: ansible_selinux is defined and ansible_selinux.status | default("disabled") == "enabled"
  failed_when: false

- name: Install Apache reverse proxy for S3 Explorer
  when: s3_explorer_ensure_httpd_proxy | default(true) | bool
  ansible.builtin.template:
    src: s3explorer-httpd.conf.j2
    dest: /etc/httpd/conf.d/zz-ansible-s3explorer.conf
    mode: "0644"
  notify: restart httpd

- name: Enable and start S3 Explorer service
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: rclone-s3-explorer.service
    enabled: true
    state: started
    daemon_reload: true

---
# Root playbook: role flags use enable_* in group_vars/<env>/roles.yml and group_vars/all/roles.yml
- name: Deploy Artelys Crystal HPC
  hosts: all
  become: true
  vars_files:
    - vault/secrets.yml

  roles:
    - role: dnf_update
      tags: [dnf_update, base]
      when: (enable_dnf_update | default(true)) | bool

    - role: common
      tags: [common, base]
      when: (enable_common | default(true)) | bool

    - role: docker
      tags: [docker, optional, container]
      when: (enable_docker | default(false)) | bool

    - role: network
      tags: [network, base]
      when: (enable_network | default(true)) | bool

    - role: storage
      tags: [storage, base]
      when: (enable_storage | default(true)) | bool

    - role: ldap
      tags: [ldap, base]
      when: (enable_ldap | default(true)) | bool

    - role: sssd
      tags: [sssd, base]
      when: (enable_sssd | default(true)) | bool

    - role: user_tools
      tags: [user_tools, base]
      when: (enable_user_tools | default(true)) | bool

    - role: slurm
      tags: [slurm, base]
      when: (enable_slurm | default(true)) | bool

    - role: open_on_demand
      tags: [open_on_demand, base, openondemand]
      when: (enable_open_on_demand | default(true)) | bool

    - role: ood_ssl
      tags: [ood_ssl, optional]
      when: (enable_ood_ssl | default(true)) | bool

    - role: jupyter
      tags: [jupyter, optional]
      when: (enable_jupyter | default(false)) | bool

    - role: desktop_vnc
      tags: [desktop_vnc, optional]
      when: (enable_desktop_vnc | default(false)) | bool

    - role: grafana
      tags: [grafana, optional]
      when: (enable_grafana | default(false)) | bool

    - role: jupyter_python313
      tags: [jupyter_python313, optional]
      when: (enable_jupyter_python313 | default(false)) | bool

    - role: selinux
      tags: [selinux, optional]
      when: (enable_selinux | default(false)) | bool

    - role: css_theme
      tags: [css_theme, optional, artelys_theme]
      when: (enable_css_theme | default(true)) | bool

    - role: s3_explorer
      tags: [s3_explorer, optional]
      when: (enable_s3_explorer | default(true)) | bool

---
# Legacy entry name — the canonical playbook is playbook.yml
- name: Artelys Crystal HPC (import)
  ansible.builtin.import_playbook: playbook.yml

---
# Default role toggles (all groups merge; per-environment group_vars/<env>/roles.yml overrides)
enable_dnf_update: true
enable_common: true
enable_docker: false
enable_network: true
enable_storage: true
enable_ldap: true
enable_sssd: true
enable_user_tools: true
enable_slurm: true
enable_open_on_demand: true
# Map legacy group_vars (install_*) to enable_* so existing inventories keep working
enable_ood_ssl: "{{ install_ood_ssl | default(true) }}"
enable_jupyter: "{{ install_jupyter | default(true) }}"
enable_desktop_vnc: "{{ install_desktop_vnc | default(false) }}"
enable_grafana: "{{ install_grafana | default(false) }}"
enable_jupyter_python313: "{{ install_python313 | default(false) }}"
enable_selinux: "{{ install_selinux | default(false) }}"
enable_css_theme: "{{ install_artelys_theme | default(true) }}"
enable_s3_explorer: "{{ install_s3_explorer | default(true) }}"

input.contents:
# Per-cluster entry points

Each subfolder is one physical or logical site (e.g. `cluster-a/`, `cluster-b/`). Place **inventory** and any **local overrides** there; run the root playbook with `-i` pointing at that inventory.

- `cluster-a/` — example layout with its own `inventory/hosts.yml` and optional `host_vars/`.
- Use `group_vars/<env>/roles.yml` at the project root for environment-wide toggles, or add `group_vars` under a deployment to scope vars to one site only (Ansible merge order still applies per host).

**Example (from the repository root):**

```bash
uv run ansible-playbook playbook.yml \
  -i deployments/cluster-a/inventory/hosts.yml \
  --vault-password-file .vault_pass
```

`vars_files: vault/secrets.yml` in `playbook.yml` is relative to the **imported** playbook on disk, so the vault file under the project root is used when the root playbook is loaded.

---
# Example: single-node HPC; copy and edit per site
all:
  children:
    hpc_servers:
      children:
        dev:
          hosts:
            hpc-ctl-01:
              ansible_host: 10.0.0.10
              ansible_user: almalinux
              ansible_become: true
        # staging: {}
        # prod: {}

# cluster-a

Cluster-specific variables can live in `host_vars/`, or inherit `group_vars` from the repository root. Run from the project root (see `deployments/README.md`).

Optional local `group_vars` example:

- `group_vars/hpc_servers.yml` — cluster-only overrides
- `group_vars/dev/roles.yml` — same pattern as the root `group_vars/<env>/roles.yml` for `enable_*` flags

---
# Example second cluster
all:
  children:
    hpc_servers:
      children:
        prod:
          hosts:
            hpc-prod-01:
              ansible_host: 10.0.1.20
              ansible_user: almalinux
              ansible_become: true

---
# Entry: run with `cd` at repo root and `-i deployments/cluster-a/inventory/hosts.yml`
- import_playbook: ../../playbook.yml

---
- import_playbook: ../../playbook.yml

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run on a machine with internet. Produces a directory suitable for copy to SecNumCloud (no GitLab, no public repos at deploy time).
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
OUT="${OFFLINE_BUNDLE_DIR:-$ROOT/.offline-bundles/bundle-$(date +%Y%m%d)}"
export ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH="${ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH:-$OUT/collections}"
mkdir -p "$OUT/collections" "$OUT/galaxy-roles" "$OUT/docs"
echo "Output: $OUT"

if ! command -v ansible-galaxy >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "install ansible in this env (e.g. uv run ansible-galaxy) before running" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Pinned collection tarballs and dependency resolution (no network on target)
cp "$ROOT/requirements.yml" "$OUT/requirements.yml"
ansible-galaxy collection download -r "$ROOT/requirements.yml" -p "$OUT/calaxy-download" || {
  # Older ansible-galaxy: install then copy from collections path
  rm -rf "$OUT/calaxy-download" 2>/dev/null || true
  ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH="$OUT/collections" ansible-galaxy collection install -r "$ROOT/requirements.yml" --force
}

# Platform git bundle (if remotes are unavailable on the server, sync config with an offline copy)
# Usage on site: git clone /path/to/platform.git.bundle platform
if [[ -d "$ROOT/.git" ]]; then
  git -C "$ROOT" bundle create "$OUT/artelys-crystal-hpc-deployment.git.bundle" --all 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Optional: mirror RPMs the roles install (rclone, podman-docker, ondemand, …) with reposync on a RHEL/Alma builder — not automated here.
cat > "$OUT/SECNUMCLOUD-README-fragment.txt" <<'EOF'
1. Copy this entire directory to the air-gapped jump host.
2. On the control node: export ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH=$PWD/collections
   and install: ansible-galaxy collection install -r requirements.yml -p collections
   (if only tarballs: ansible-galaxy collection install *.tar.gz -p collections)
3. Set local_repo_path / pre-cloned local_repo_dest for roles that use git, or use git clone from a .git.bundle.
4. See README_SECNUMCLOUD.md in the project root for full runbook.
EOF
echo "Done. See $OUT/SECNUMCLOUD-README-fragment.txt"
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run on a machine with internet. Produces a directory to copy to SecNumCloud (no network at deploy time).
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
OUT="${OFFLINE_BUNDLE_DIR:-$ROOT/.offline-bundles/bundle-$(date +%Y%m%d)}"
mkdir -p "$OUT/collection-files" "$OUT/collections" "$OUT/docs"
echo "Output: $OUT"
cp -f "$ROOT/requirements.yml" "$OUT/requirements.yml"

if ! command -v ansible-galaxy >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Use project venv, e.g.: (cd $ROOT && uv run bash scripts/prepare_offline_bundle.sh)" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# ansible-galaxy collection download — writes tarballs (Ansible 2.10+)
if ansible-galaxy collection download -h 2>&1 | grep -q "download" && \
  ansible-galaxy collection download -r "$ROOT/requirements.yml" -p "$OUT/collection-files"; then
  :
else
  ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH="$OUT/collections" ansible-galaxy collection install -r "$ROOT/requirements.yml" --force
fi

if [[ -d "$ROOT/.git" ]]; then
  git -C "$ROOT" bundle create "$OUT/artelys-crystal-hpc-deployment.git.bundle" --all 2>/dev/null || true
fi

cat > "$OUT/SECNUMCLOUD-README-fragment.txt" <<'EOF'
1. Copy this directory to the air-gapped control host.
2. Install collections: ansible-galaxy collection install -r requirements.yml -p /path/to/collections
   or from local tarballs: ansible-galaxy collection install /path/*.tar.gz -p /path/to/collections
3. export ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH=/path/to/collections
4. Point local_repo at a git bundle or extracted tree for roles that use git; see README_SECNUMCLOUD.md
EOF
echo "Done. Read $OUT/SECNUMCLOUD-README-fragment.txt"
exit 0

---
# Pin collection versions for reproducible offline bundles (prepare_offline_bundle.sh)
collections:
  - name: community.mysql
    version: 3.14.0
  - name: ansible.posix
    version: 2.1.0
  - name: community.general
    version: 11.2.0

# SecNumCloud / air-gapped deployment

This project normally pulls **Ansible Galaxy collections**, **DNF/RPM** packages, **Open OnDemand** RPMs from OSC, and optionally a **Git** config repository. In SecNumCloud there is no internet and often no Artelys GitLab — you must **stage all artifacts on a build host with connectivity**, then transfer a **single bundle** to the control node that runs Ansible.

## Strategy (no network on the control node at deploy time)

1. **Collections** — Pin versions in `requirements.yml`. On a connected machine run `scripts/prepare_offline_bundle.sh` (or manually `ansible-galaxy collection download -r requirements.yml` / `install` into a known directory). Copy the resulting `collection-files/` directory or the expanded `collections/` tree plus `requirements.yml` to the air gap. On the target control host set:
   - `export ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH=/opt/ansible/collections` (or your path)
   - or install with `ansible-galaxy collection install *.tar.gz -p "$ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH"`.

2. **This repository** — Optional: `git bundle` (the script can emit `artelys-crystal-hpc-deployment.git.bundle`) or a tarball of the tree; the playbook does not pull from the network if all paths are local.

3. **Platform / OOD config git (`local_repo_url`)** — Mirror the `platform` repo to internal storage, or `git clone` on a connected box, `git bundle create platform.git.bundle --all`, and on SecNumCloud `git clone platform.git.bundle` to a path; set:
   - `local_repo_url: file:///opt/repositories/platform` (or `copy:` from a vendor tarball in a custom pre-task — not in this role by default).

4. **Open OnDemand / Slurm / EPEL / CRB RPMs** — Mirror required repos with `reposync` or `dnf download --resolve` on AlmaLinux 9, or vendor ISOs, and point `roles/open_on_demand` to **local** `dnf` repositories (`dnf: name: /path/*.rpm` pattern is not in-tree; use a local repo `.repo` under `/etc/yum.repos.d/` for offline). The `docker` role supports `docker_local_rpm_glob` for a glob of pre-staged RPMs. The `s3_explorer` role installs `rclone` from dnf; add `rclone` to your offline base repo.

5. **No Galaxy / Git at runtime** — Do not run `ansible-galaxy install` on the air-gapped control node without local sources. Do not set `http(s)` `local_repo_url` for `git` unless a proxy to an internal Git mirror is available.

## Minimal checklist before running `ansible-playbook`

- [ ] `ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH` populated from the offline collection bundle
- [ ] DNF (or dnf5) can install packages from on-prem mirrors only
- [ ] OOD/Slurm/Rclone/Jupyter/… RPMs are available on those mirrors
- [ ] `platform` config tree is available at `local_repo_dest` (git clone, rsync, or copy module from bundle)
- [ ] `vault/secrets.yml` and inventory present on the control host

## Helper script

- `bash scripts/prepare_offline_bundle.sh` — from a **connected** environment, downloads Galaxy dependencies and optionally builds a `git` bundle of this project.

## Related variables

- `local_repo_url`, `local_repo_tag` — ensure the server can `git` from this ref without GitLab.
- `docker_local_rpm_glob` — optional glob for container RPMs in air-gapped sites (see `roles/docker/defaults/main.yml`).

# Changelog

## [Unreleased]

### Added

- Root `playbook.yml` with `enable_*` role toggles; `group_vars/<env>/roles.yml` for dev, staging, prod.
- Roles: `docker`, `dnf_update`; `open_on_demand` (renamed from `openondemand`), `css_theme` (renamed from `artelys_theme`).
- Implement `s3_explorer` (rclone rcd + Apache).
- `deployments/cluster-a`, `deployments/cluster-b` examples; `scripts/prepare_offline_bundle.sh`, `README_SECNUMCLOUD.md`.
- Pinned `requirements.yml` for offline builds.

### Changed

- `site.yml` now imports `playbook.yml`.
- Inventory: `hpc_servers` and env groups `dev`, `staging`, `prod` (replaces `dev_servers` / `demo_servers` / `prod_servers` as top-level group names; hosts unchanged).

# artelys-crystal-hpc-deployment — Ansible spec audit

## Goal

Align the Artelys Crystal HPC Ansible repo with the team spec: required roles (snake_case), root `playbook.yml` with `enable_*` toggles, per-env `group_vars/<env>/roles.yml`, `deployments/` layout, SecNumCloud offline strategy.

## What changed

- Added roles: `dnf_update`, `docker`; renamed `openondemand` → `open_on_demand`, `artelys_theme` → `css_theme`.
- Implemented `s3_explorer` (rclone rcd + Apache).
- Root `playbook.yml` with `when: enable_*`; `site.yml` imports it; `group_vars/all.yml` maps `install_*` → `enable_*`.
- Inventory: `hpc_servers` + `dev` / `staging` / `prod`; `group_vars/{dev,staging,prod}/roles.yml` examples.
- `deployments/cluster-a|b/` with inventory + `site.yml` import; `scripts/prepare_offline_bundle.sh`; `README_SECNUMCLOUD.md`; pinned `requirements.yml`; `CHANGELOG.md`.
- CI: `ansible-lint -P min` on playbooks; deploy uses `playbook.yml`.

## Commands

- `ansible-playbook --syntax-check site.yml`
- `uv run --with ansible-lint ansible-lint -P min playbook.yml site.yml`
- `ansible-galaxy collection install -r requirements.yml` (pins verified)

## Follow-ups

- Optional: tighten ansible-lint profile beyond `min` and fix FQCN across legacy roles.
- Confirm `podman.socket` + `podman-docker` matches target policy; override with `docker_local_rpm_glob` for docker-ce offline.

## Audit vs specification

| Requirement | Verdict | Notes |
|---------------|---------|--------|
| **`docker` role** | Was **missing** — now **implemented** | `roles/docker/` installs `podman-docker` by default, `podman.socket`, optional `docker_local_rpm_glob` for offline RPMs. |
| **`open_on_demand` role (snake_case)** | Was **wrong name** (`openondemand`) — **renamed** | Content is the former `roles/openondemand/` → `roles/open_on_demand/` with `meta/`, `defaults/`. |
| **`s3_explorer` role** | Was **placeholder only** — **implemented** | rclone `rcd` + web UI, Apache `ProxyPass`, `roles/s3_explorer/{tasks,templates,defaults,handlers,meta,files}/`. |
| **`css_theme` role** | Was **`artelys_theme`** — **renamed** to match spec | Same tasks as before, `roles/css_theme/`. |
| **`dnf_update` role** | **Missing** — **added** | `roles/dnf_update/` full system or security-only update. |
| **Root `playbook.yml` + conditional roles** | Was only `site.yml` with `install_*` — **added** | `playbook.yml` uses `when: (enable_* \| default(...)) \| bool`. |
| **Per-env activation via `enable_*` + `group_vars/<env>/roles.yml`** | **Missing** — **added** | `group_vars/all.yml` defines defaults; `group_vars/{dev,staging,prod}/roles.yml`; legacy `install_*` in `hpc_servers.yml` still mapped to `enable_*`. |
| **`deployments/<cluster>/` layout** | **Missing** — **added** | `deployments/cluster-a`, `cluster-b` with inventory + `site.yml` → `../../playbook.yml`. |
| **SecNumCloud offline strategy** | **Missing** — **added** | Pinned `requirements.yml`, `scripts/prepare_offline_bundle.sh`, `README_SECNUMCLOUD.md`, `docker_local_rpm_glob`, role comments. |
| **Ansible conventions (defaults, meta, handlers, templates, files)** | **Partial** — **addressed** | New/renamed roles have the expected layout; `defaults/main.yml` added for OOD/css where useful; empty `files/.gitkeep` where no static files. |

**Still true from the original project (unchanged behaviour you can rely on):** `bootstrap.yml`, vault pattern, core stack roles (`common` through `slurm`), Molecule layout, and most optional roles are as before aside from naming and toggles.

**Verification:** `ansible-playbook --syntax-check playbook.yml` and `site.yml` exit 0. CI uses `ansible-lint -P min` on `site.yml`, `bootstrap.yml`, `playbook.yml` (full `production` profile still flags many legacy roles for FQCN; that predates this change).

**Brain sync:** session `end` failed (stale `index.lock` on the vault); if you use brain-sync, remove the lock or retry when no other git process is using the vault.

---

Below: **reason + full content** for the main new/updated artefacts. Other paths (e.g. every template under `roles/s3_explorer/templates/`) are in the repo unchanged from what we wrote.

---

### `playbook.yml`
**Reason:** Canonical root playbook with `enable_*` booleans per role.

```yaml
---
# Root playbook: role flags use enable_* in group_vars/<env>/roles.yml and group_vars/all/roles.yml
- name: Deploy Artelys Crystal HPC
  hosts: all
  become: true
  vars_files:
    - vault/secrets.yml

  roles:
    - role: dnf_update
      tags: [dnf_update, base]
      when: (enable_dnf_update | default(true)) | bool

    - role: common
      tags: [common, base]
      when: (enable_common | default(true)) | bool

    - role: docker
      tags: [docker, optional, container]
      when: (enable_docker | default(false)) | bool

    - role: network
      tags: [network, base]
      when: (enable_network | default(true)) | bool

    - role: storage
      tags: [storage, base]
      when: (enable_storage | default(true)) | bool

    - role: ldap
      tags: [ldap, base]
      when: (enable_ldap | default(true)) | bool

    - role: sssd
      tags: [sssd, base]
      when: (enable_sssd | default(true)) | bool

    - role: user_tools
      tags: [user_tools, base]
      when: (enable_user_tools | default(true)) | bool

    - role: slurm
      tags: [slurm, base]
      when: (enable_slurm | default(true)) | bool

    - role: open_on_demand
      tags: [open_on_demand, base, openondemand]
      when: (enable_open_on_demand | default(true)) | bool

    - role: ood_ssl
      tags: [ood_ssl, optional]
      when: (enable_ood_ssl | default(false)) | bool

    - role: jupyter
      tags: [jupyter, optional]
      when: (enable_jupyter | default(false)) | bool

    - role: desktop_vnc
      tags: [desktop_vnc, optional]
      when: (enable_desktop_vnc | default(false)) | bool

    - role: grafana
      tags: [grafana, optional]
      when: (enable_grafana | default(false)) | bool

    - role: jupyter_python313
      tags: [jupyter_python313, optional]
      when: (enable_jupyter_python313 | default(false)) | bool

    - role: selinux
      tags: [selinux, optional]
      when: (enable_selinux | default(false)) | bool

    - role: css_theme
      tags: [css_theme, optional, artelys_theme]
      when: (enable_css_theme | default(false)) | bool

    - role: s3_explorer
      tags: [s3_explorer, optional]
      when: (enable_s3_explorer | default(false)) | bool

```

### `requirements.yml`
**Reason:** Pinned collections for reproducible offline bundles.

```yaml

collections:
  - name: community.mysql
    version: 3.14.0
  - name: ansible.posix

    version: 11.2.0

```

### `scripts/prepare_offline_bundle.sh`
**Reason:** Pre-download Galaxy artifacts (and optional git bundle) on a connected host.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run on a machine with internet. Produces a directory to copy to SecNumCloud (no network at deploy time).
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
OUT="${OFFLINE_BUNDLE_DIR:-$ROOT/.offline-bundles/bundle-$(date +%Y%m%d)}"
mkdir -p "$OUT/collection-files" "$OUT/collections" "$OUT/docs"
echo "Output: $OUT"
cp -f "$ROOT/requirements.yml" "$OUT/requirements.yml"

if ! command -v ansible-galaxy >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Use project venv, e.g.: (cd $ROOT && uv run bash scripts/prepare_offline_bundle.sh)" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# ansible-galaxy collection download — writes tarballs (Ansible 2.10+)
if ansible-galaxy collection download -h 2>&1 | grep -q "download" && \
  ansible-galaxy collection download -r "$ROOT/requirements.yml" -p "$OUT/collection-files"; then
  :
else
  ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH="$OUT/collections" ansible-galaxy collection install -r "$ROOT/requirements.yml" --force
fi

if [[ -d "$ROOT/.git" ]]; then
  git -C "$ROOT" bundle create "$OUT/artelys-crystal-hpc-deployment.git.bundle" --all 2>/dev/null || true
fi

cat > "$OUT/SECNUMCLOUD-README-fragment.txt" <<'EOF'
1. Copy this directory to the air-gapped control host.
2. Install collections: ansible-galaxy collection install -r requirements.yml -p /path/to/collections
   or from local tarballs: ansible-galaxy collection install /path/*.tar.gz -p /path/to/collections
3. export ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH=/path/to/collections
4. Point local_repo at a git bundle or extracted tree for roles that use git; see README_SECNUMCLOUD.md
EOF
echo "Done. Read $OUT/SECNUMCLOUD-README-fragment.txt"
exit 0

```

*(Full text of `README_SECNUMCLOUD.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `roles/dnf_update/**`, `roles/docker/**`, `roles/s3_explorer/**`, `deployments/**/site.yml`, etc., is in the tree; if you want any single file pasted in full in chat, say which path.)*

Roles Content
roles/update/tasks/main.yml
---
- name: Update all packages with DNF
  ansible.builtin.dnf:
    name: "*"
    state: latest
    update_cache: true
  notify: Reboot if kernel updated

- name: Install common utilities
  ansible.builtin.dnf:
    name:
      - curl
      - wget
      - vim
      - git
      - net-tools
    state: present
roles/update/handlers/main.yml
---
- name: Reboot if kernel updated
  ansible.builtin.reboot:
    reboot_timeout: 300

roles/docker/tasks/main.yml
---
- name: Remove old Docker versions
  ansible.builtin.dnf:
    name:
      - docker
      - docker-client
      - docker-common
    state: absent

- name: Add Docker repository
  ansible.builtin.yum_repository:
    name: docker-ce
    description: Docker CE Stable
    baseurl: "https://download.docker.com/linux/centos/$releasever/$basearch/stable"
    gpgkey: "https://download.docker.com/linux/centos/gpg"
    gpgcheck: true
    enabled: true

- name: Install Docker packages
  ansible.builtin.dnf:
    name:
      - docker-ce
      - docker-ce-cli
      - containerd.io
      - docker-buildx-plugin
      - docker-compose-plugin
    state: present
  notify: Start and enable Docker

- name: Deploy Docker daemon config
  ansible.builtin.template:
    src: daemon.json.j2
    dest: /etc/docker/daemon.json
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: '0644'
  notify: Restart Docker
roles/docker/handlers/main.yml
---
- name: Start and enable Docker
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: docker
    state: started
    enabled: true

- name: Restart Docker
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: docker
    state: restarted
roles/docker/templates/daemon.json.j2
{
  "storage-driver": "{{ docker_storage_driver | default('overlay2') }}",
  "log-driver": "json-file",
  "log-opts": {
    "max-size": "100m",
    "max-file": "3"
  }
}

roles/slurm/tasks/main.yml
---
- name: Install Slurm packages
  ansible.builtin.dnf:
    name:
      - slurm
      - slurm-slurmctld
      - slurm-slurmd
      - munge
    state: present

- name: Deploy Slurm configuration
  ansible.builtin.template:
    src: slurm.conf.j2
    dest: /etc/slurm/slurm.conf
    owner: slurm
    group: slurm
    mode: '0644'
  notify: Restart Slurm

- name: Configure master node
  ansible.builtin.import_tasks: master.yml
  when: inventory_hostname in groups['masters']

- name: Configure worker nodes
  ansible.builtin.import_tasks: worker.yml
  when: inventory_hostname in groups['workers']
roles/slurm/tasks/master.yml
---
- name: Enable and start slurmctld on master
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: slurmctld
    state: started
    enabled: true
roles/slurm/tasks/worker.yml
---
- name: Enable and start slurmd on workers
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: slurmd
    state: started
    enabled: true

-# Artelys Crystal HPC — Ansible Deployment
-
-Turns a bare AlmaLinux 9 server into a self-contained HPC cluster: LDAP user directory (389-ds + SSSD), Slurm workload manager with MariaDB accounting, and an Open OnDemand web portal — all on a single node acting simultaneously as controller, compute node, login node, and web front-end.
-
-Optional add-ons (Jupyter kernels, Xfce/VNC desktop, Grafana + Prometheus, S3 file browser, Artelys branding) are toggled via feature flags. The playbook is fully idempotent and can be driven from a local workstation or a GitLab CI pipeline.
-
----
-
-## Ansible concepts used in this repo
-
-| Concept | What it is | Where it lives |
-|---------|-----------|----------------|
-| **Inventory** | Lists the target servers and groups them (dev, demo, prod) | `inventory/hosts.yml` |
-| **Playbook** | YAML file mapping host groups to an ordered list of roles | `playbook.yml` (canonical), `site.yml` (imports it), `bootstrap.yml` |
-| **Role** | Reusable component bundling tasks, templates, handlers, and defaults for one concern (e.g. `slurm`, `ldap`) | `roles/<name>/` |
-| **Task** | A single action inside a role — calls one module with specific arguments (e.g. "ensure firewalld is started") | `roles/<name>/tasks/main.yml` |
-| **Module** | Built-in Ansible tool that performs a specific operation: `dnf` installs packages, `systemd` manages services, `template` renders Jinja2 files, `user` manages accounts | used inside tasks |
-| **Variables** | Named values that customise behaviour across environments (versions, hostnames, feature flags) | `group_vars/all.yml`, `group_vars/hpc_servers.yml` |
-| **Facts** | System information Ansible gathers automatically at runtime (OS, IPs, disk) — available as variables in any task | gathered automatically |
-| **Vault** | Encrypted file for secrets (passwords, TLS keys) — decrypted transparently at runtime | `vault/secrets.yml` |
-| **Tags** | Labels on roles/tasks to run or skip subsets of the playbook | `--tags slurm`, `--skip-tags optional` |
-| **Handlers** | Tasks that fire exactly once when notified — used to restart a service only if its config changed | `roles/<name>/handlers/main.yml` |
-| **Idempotence** | Core philosophy: every task checks current state first and only acts if a change is needed — safe to re-run at any time | enforced by all modules |
-
----
-
-**Target OS:** AlmaLinux 9  
-**Managed component versions** (set in `group_vars/all.yml`):
-
-| Component | Version |
-|-----------|---------|
-| Slurm | 25.05.3 |
-| Munge | 0.5.16 |
-| Open OnDemand | 3.1 |
-
----
-
-## What the playbook does
-
-The playbook deploys a single-node HPC server acting simultaneously as controller, compute node, login node, and web portal. It maps directly to the original numbered shell scripts:
-
-| Role | Original scripts | What it does |
-|------|-----------------|--------------|
-| `common` | `01-configure-server.sh` | EPEL, base packages, firewalld, timezone |
-| `network` | `02-configure-network.sh` | Adds `CTL1` as alias `127.0.0.2` |
-| `storage` | `05-install-raid6.sh`, `06-install-storages.sh` | `/data` mount and remote home setup |
-| `ldap` | `07-install-ldap.sh` | 389-ds instance, base DN, SSSD bind account |
-| `sssd` | `08-install-sssd.sh` | SSSD, PAM, NSS configuration |
-| `user_tools` | `09-install-users-cmd.sh` | `hpc-createUser`, `hpc-addUserToGroup`, `hpc-modifyPassword` scripts |
-| `slurm` | `03-install-slurm.sh`, `04-configure-slurm.sh` | Build munge + slurm from source via `rpmbuild`, MariaDB for `slurmdbd`, cluster registration |
-| `open_on_demand` | `10-install-ondemand.sh`, `12-install-ondemand-reload.sh` | Open OnDemand portal, Apache config |
-| `ood_ssl` *(optional)* | `11-install-ondemand-ssl.sh` | Self-signed or provided TLS certificate |
-| `jupyter` *(optional)* | `13-install-jupyter.sh` | System Python 3.9 Jupyter kernel |
-| `desktop_vnc` *(optional)* | `14-install-desktop-vnc.sh` | Xfce + VNC server |
-| `grafana` *(optional)* | `15-install-grafana.sh` | Grafana + Prometheus + SLURM dashboard |
-| `jupyter_python313` *(optional)* | `16-jupyter-python3.13.sh` | Python 3.13 Jupyter kernel |
-| `selinux` *(optional)* | `17-selinux-support.sh` | SELinux policy adjustments |
-| `css_theme` *(optional)* | `18-install-artelys-theme.sh` | Artelys CSS / branding on OOD and Dex |
-| `s3_explorer` *(optional)* | — | S3 web UI (rclone rcd) behind Apache |
-
-Optional roles are gated by feature flags in `group_vars/hpc_servers.yml` (see [Enabling optional components](#enabling-optional-components)).
-

-
-## Repository layout
-
-```
-artelys-crystal-hpc-deployment/
-├── bootstrap.yml              # One-shot OS prep (hostname, firewall, base packages)
-├── playbook.yml               # Root playbook — conditionally runs roles via enable_* flags
-├── site.yml                   # Imports playbook.yml (backward-compatible entry)

-│   ├── staging/roles.yml

-├── roles/

+# Artelys Crystal HPC — Ansible Project
+
+This repository is now organized around a strict deployment structure:
+
+- `ansible.cfg`
+- `playbooks/` (`playbook.yml`, `update.yml`, `docker.yml`, `slurm.yml`)
+- `deployments/hpc_dev`, `deployments/hpc_solution_demo`, `deployments/coe2`
+- `roles/update`, `roles/docker`, `roles/slurm`, `roles/open_on_demand`, `roles/s3_explorer`, `roles/theme`
+
+All three deployments have the same role activation (`enable_* = true` for every role), with cluster-specific values for hostnames, URLs, bucket names, and theme settings.
+
+## Old scripts coverage
+
+The old shell scripts under `old_scripts/` are covered through role composition:
+
+- `01` + `02` + `05` + `06` + `07` + `08` + `09` via `roles/update` (imports `common`, `network`, `storage`, `ldap`, `sssd`, `user_tools`).
